## **CLINICAL LABORATORY SERVICES & REFERRAL AGREEMENT** ## **SPECIALIZED REPRODUCTIVE ENDOCRINOLOGY & INFERTILITY FRAMEWORK** This Clinical Laboratory Services & Referral Agreement (the " **Agreement** ") is executed and made effective as of **June 22, 2026** (the " **Effective Date** "), by and between the following medical entities: **==> picture [512 x 31] intentionally omitted <==** **----- Start of picture text -----**
THE FERTILITY CENTER THE LABORATORY
**----- End of picture text -----**

|**THE FERTILITY CENTER**|**THE LABORATORY**| |---|---| |**Aura Reproductive Health & Fertility Clinic**
Suite 450, Medical Plaza Tower
880 Pipette Boulevard, Innovation District
Chennai, TN 600096
Represented by its Clinical Practice Director|**Nexus Diagnostics & Bio-Analytics Corp.**
Building C, Biotech Park East
124 Century Science Highway, Sector 4
Chennai, TN 600119
Represented by its Laboratory Director|

**WHEREAS,** the Fertility Center provides comprehensive reproductive endocrinology, advanced infertility care, and clinical assessments requiring specialized and routine laboratory diagnostic testing; and 

**WHEREAS,** the Laboratory is clinically certified, licensed, and equipped to execute standard and highly complex diagnostic and screening assays and agrees to perform such services for referred patients under strict clinical standards;

**NOW,** in consideration of the mutual clinical covenants, obligations, and terms set forth below, the Parties **THEREFORE,** agree to the following ten (10) clauses: 

## **1. Referral Framework & Preferred Vendor Designation**

The Fertility Center hereby designates the Laboratory as its preferred provider for general and routine diagnostic laboratory workups. The Fertility Center shall route patient clinical test orders (including routine blood, endocrine panels,  metabolic  screens,  and  initial  diagnostic  requisitions)  directly  to  the  Laboratory  through  an  integrated Electronic Health Record (EHR) referral interface or authorized clinical requisitions. 

## **2. Specialized Laboratory Capabilities & Testing Suites**

The Laboratory covenants that it maintains the requisite equipment, reagents, and certified medical technologists to perform standard routine clinical diagnostics, specialized endocrine assays (including but not limited to _**AMH**_ , _**FSH**_ , _**LH**_ , and _**Beta-hCG**_ ), toxicology panels, and foundational male fertility screens (including semen analysis collection and baseline processing) according to recognized medical guidelines. 

## **3. Turnaround Time (TAT) & Priority Processing**

## **3. Turnaround Time (TAT) & Priority Processing** 

Due to the critical, time-sensitive nature of cycle-dependent fertility treatments, the Laboratory guarantees prioritized processing for all samples received from the Fertility Center. Standard quantitative hormone assays and critical blood chemistries must yield verified digital results within a maximum of four (4) to six (6) hours from sample collection, while baseline routine diagnostics must be delivered within twenty-four (24) hours. 

**CONFIDENTIAL & PROPRIETARY**

**CONFIDENTIAL & PROPRIETARY** 

Page 1 of 3 

## **4. Specimen Collection, Handling, & Chain of Custody** 

The  Laboratory  shall  supply  all  necessary  collection  vials,  specialized  transport  media,  and  standard  handling instructions to ensure specimen viability. Both parties agree to strictly document and maintain a flawless, airtight clinical chain of custody protocol for all blood, serum, and diagnostic fluid specimens, minimizing the risk of contamination, mislabeling, or degradation. 

## **5. Electronic Medical Records (EMR) Integration & Result Reporting**

The Parties shall implement and maintain a secure, encrypted digital interface (such as an HL7-compliant or equivalent secure connection) to link the Laboratory’s Information System (LIS) directly to the Fertility Center’s Electronic Medical Record (EMR). Final verified test results must be automatically pushed to the ordering provider’s dashboard immediately upon clinical verification. 

## **6. Patient Billing, Insurance Coordination, & Corporate Rates**

Billing shall be processed via a "Bill to Patient" or "Bill to Third-Party Payer/Insurance" framework, where the Laboratory handles direct financial clearance with the patient or their insurance provider at the time of intake. The Laboratory agrees to extend a specialized corporate health rate schedule to self-pay patients referred by the Fertility Center, minimizing out-of-pocket costs. 

## **7. Medical Confidentiality & Data Protection**

## **7. Medical Confidentiality & Data Protection** 

The Laboratory shall treat all diagnostic findings, pathology histories, and patient demographic records with absolute clinical confidentiality. Data storage, electronic transmission, and record retention must strictly comply with all applicable local, regional, and federal medical privacy regulations governing protected health information. 

## **8. Quality Assurance, Licensing, & Clinical Audits**

## **8. Quality Assurance, Licensing, & Clinical Audits** 

The Laboratory warrants that it holds and will maintain all necessary state, federal, and local medical laboratory licenses and certifications required to operate a diagnostic facility. The Laboratory grants the Fertility Center the right to conduct an annual clinical quality audit of testing facilities, verification logs, and proficiency testing data to ensure continuous alignment with fertility care standards. 

## **9. Term, Automatic Renewal, & Modifications**

## **9. Term, Automatic Renewal, & Modifications** 

This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and remain in full force for an initial term of twelve (12) months. Upon expiration of the initial term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive twelve-month periods unless either party delivers a written notice of modification or non-renewal at least sixty (60) days prior to the upcoming renewal date. 

## **10. Indemnification & Termination Protocol**

## **10. Indemnification & Termination Protocol** 

Either party may terminate this arrangement without cause by transmitting a formal sixty (60) day advance written notification. Furthermore, either party may terminate this agreement immediately for cause if clinical licenses are suspended, or if a material breach of confidentiality occurs. Each party agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the other from liabilities arising directly from its own clinical negligence or operational errors. 

**CONFIDENTIAL & PROPRIETARY**
